From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

United States Navy vessel

For other ships going by the same name, see [USS Gazelle](/wiki/USS_Gazelle "USS Gazelle").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
|  | |
|  | |
| History | |
|  | |
| United States | |
| Name | * *Cyrus K. Holliday* * *Gazelle* |
| Namesake | * [Cyrus K. Holliday](/wiki/Cyrus_K._Holliday "Cyrus K. Holliday") * The [gazelle](/wiki/Gazelle "Gazelle") |
| Launched | 9 November 1943 |

| Beam | 56 ft 11 in (17.35 m) |
| Draught | 28 ft 4 in (8.64 m) |
| Speed | 11.5 knots |
| Complement | 115 officers and men |
| Armament | one five-inch (127 mm) gun |
|  | |

**USS *Gazelle* (IX-116)**, an [unclassified miscellaneous vessel](/wiki/Unclassified_miscellaneous_vessel "Unclassified miscellaneous vessel"), was the third ship of the [United States Navy](/wiki/United_States_Navy "United States Navy") to be named for the [gazelle](/wiki/Gazelle "Gazelle"), any of numerous small, graceful, and swift [antelopes](/wiki/Antelope "Antelope"), with lustrous eyes, found especially in [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa"), northern Africa, [Iran](/wiki/Iran "Iran"), and [India](/wiki/India "India"). She was built as an auxiliary oiler, [launched](/wiki/Ship_naming_and_launching "Ship naming and launching") on 9 November 1943 by the [California Shipbuilding Corporation](/wiki/California_Shipbuilding_Corporation "California Shipbuilding Corporation"), at [Wilmington, Los Angeles](/wiki/Wilmington,_Los_Angeles "Wilmington, Los Angeles"), as SS *Cyrus K. Holliday* sponsored by Mrs. J. E. Stewart, and acquired by the Navy and simultaneously [commissioned](/wiki/Ship_commissioning "Ship commissioning") on 29 November 1943.

*Gazelle* sailed from Wilmington on 9 January 1944 for [Pearl Harbor](/wiki/Pearl_Harbor "Pearl Harbor"), where she was assigned to Rear Admiral Turner's Southern Attack Force for the imminent assault on [Kwajalein](/wiki/Kwajalein "Kwajalein"). Underway with the task force 28 January, she entered Kwajalein Lagoon on 2 February 1944 and began fueling operations in the midst of the furious fighting. For two weeks she fueled combatant ships, including battleships
[*New Mexico* (BB-40)](/wiki/USS_New_Mexico_(BB-40) "USS New Mexico (BB-40)"),
[*Mississippi* (BB-23)](/wiki/USS_Mississippi_(BB-23) "USS Mississippi (BB-23)"),
[*Idaho* (BB-42)](/wiki/USS_Idaho_(BB-42) "USS Idaho (BB-42)"),
many [cruisers](/wiki/Cruiser "Cruiser"), [escort carriers](/wiki/Escort_carriers "Escort carriers"), and smaller warships.

Her mission accomplished, *Gazelle* stood out 15 February for further combat duties. She steamed into [Eniwetok](/wiki/Eniwetok "Eniwetok") Lagoon on 18 February to support the amphibious invasion of that strategic island. Here in the front line of fighting she fueled several [destroyer](/wiki/Destroyer "Destroyer") divisions before sailing 26 February for Kwajalein, where she issued fuel to American warships until getting underway 1 April for Pearl Harbor, arriving 12 April.

One week later she sailed for [Majuro](/wiki/Majuro "Majuro"), arriving 27 April, and subsequently continued fueling operations and shuttle runs through [submarine](/wiki/Submarine "Submarine")-infested waters to [Majuro](/wiki/Majuro "Majuro"), [Roi](/wiki/Roi-Namur "Roi-Namur"), [Kwajalein](/wiki/Kwajalein "Kwajalein"), and [Eniwetok](/wiki/Eniwetok "Eniwetok") through the summer of 1944, until reaching [Ulithi](/wiki/Ulithi "Ulithi") on 15 October. *Gazelle* escaped damage when Japanese midget submarines penetrated the harbor net and patrol defenses at Ulithi on 20 November and sank oiler *Mississinewa*, moored 2,000 yards (1,800 m) away. Underway once more on 10 January 1945, the ship reached [Palau](/wiki/Palau "Palau") two days later and served there until arriving [Leyte](/wiki/Leyte_Island "Leyte Island") on 28 February. While at Leyte she fueled some 100 Allied ships during the next six months.

*Gazelle* sailed 13 September for [Korea](/wiki/Korea "Korea"), arriving [Jinsen](/wiki/Jinsen "Jinsen") on 28 September, and joined the Korean Service Group, [Seventh Fleet](/wiki/US_7th_Fleet "US 7th Fleet"). She remained in Korea through October 1945.

Sometime during this period, the *Gazelle* encountered a typhoon, keeling so far over she almost sank, according to Jere Hamill, one of the ship's officers (in an oral interview with Bruce Edlen, April, 2010).

*Gazelle* reached [Norfolk, Virginia](/wiki/Norfolk,_Virginia "Norfolk, Virginia"), on 28 February 1946 and [decommissioned](/wiki/Ship_decommissioning "Ship decommissioning") there on 9 May 1946. Returned to the [War Shipping Administration](/wiki/War_Shipping_Administration "War Shipping Administration") the next day, she was stricken from the [Naval Vessel Register](/wiki/Naval_Vessel_Register "Naval Vessel Register") on 21 May 1946. In 1948 she was sold to [Intercontinental S.S. Corporation](/w/index.php?title=Intercontinental_S.S._Corporation&action=edit&redlink=1 "Intercontinental S.S. Corporation (page does not exist)") and renamed *Evistar*.

*Gazelle* earned one [battle star](/wiki/Battle_star "Battle star") for [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") service.
